lines.en_lit: a robber [ḥamtzan] until the day of his death.

Rabba bar bar Sheila said: What is the verse “O, my God, rescue me out of the hand of wicked, out of the hand of the unrighteous and robbing man [ḥometz]” Rava said: From here: “Learn to do well, seek justice, strengthen the robbed [ḥamotz]” strengthen the robbed, but not strengthen the robber.

The Sages taught: the year in which Shimon HaTzaddik died, he said to them, In this year, he will die, They said to him: How do you know? He said to them: on every Yom Kippur, I was met, by an old man who was dressed in white, and wrapped up in white, he would enter with me, and he would leave with me. But today, I was met by an old man who was dressed in black, and wrapped up in black, he entered with me, he did not leave with me. after the festival he was ill for seven days and died.
his brethren, the priests, refrained from blessing with the name of God
The Sages taught: forty years prior to the destruction of the Temple, lot did not arise in the right the strip of crimson did not turn white, and the westernmost lamp did not burn

And the doors of the Sanctuary opened by themselves until Rabban Yoḥanan ben Zakkai scolded them. He said to Sanctuary, Sanctuary, why do you frighten yourself I know about you that you will ultimately be destroyed, and Zechariah, son of Ido, has already prophesied concerning you: “Open your doors, O Lebanon, that the fire may devour your cedars”
Rabbi Yitzḥak ben Tavlai said: Why is called Lebanon [Levanon]? Because it whitens [malbin] the Jewish people’s sins,

Rav Zutra bar Toviya said: Why is called: Forest, as it is written: “The house of the forest of Lebanon” To tell you: Just as a forest blooms, so too the Temple blooms. As Rav Hoshaya said: When Solomon built the Temple, he planted in it all kinds of sweet fruit of gold, and brought forth fruit in their season. And when the wind blew upon them, their fruit would fall off, as it is stated: “May his fruits rustle like Lebanon” And through there was a source of income for the priesthood.

But once the nations entered the Sanctuary withered, as it states “And the blossoms of Lebanon wither” And in the future the Holy One, Blessed be He, will restore to us as it is stated: “It shall blossom abundantly, it shall also rejoice and shout, the glory of Lebanon will be given to it”

places upon the two goats. the Sages taught The High Priest mentions the name ten times on that day: Three during the first confession; and three during the second confession, and three the scapegoat and one with the lots,
And there already said the name his voice was heard in Jericho. Rabba bar bar Ḥana said: from Jerusalem to Jericho is ten parasangs.

And the doors of the Sanctuary was heard eight Shabbat limits, goats that were in Jericho would sneeze from the fragrance of the incense women that were in Jericho did not need to perfume themselves, fragrance incense, a bride that was in Jerusalem did not need to adorn herself fragrance incense,

Rabbi Yosei ben Dolgai said: Father had goats in the hills of Mikhmar, and they would sneeze from the fragrance of the incense. Rabbi Ḥiyya bar Avin said Rabbi Yehoshua ben Korḥa said: An old man reported to me: One time I went to Shiloh, and I smelled the smell of the incense from between its walls.
Rabbi Yannai said: drawing of the lot from inside the receptacle is an indispensable placing is not indispensable. And Rabbi Yoḥanan said: Even drawing of the lots is not indispensable,

In accordance with Rabbi Yehuda, who said matters that are performed in white garments outside are not indispensable, everyone agrees that is not indispensable, When they disagree, it is in accordance with Rabbi Neḥemya. The one who said is indispensable in accordance with Rabbi Neḥemya. And the one who said is not indispensable this applies to service. drawing of the lots is not service,
Some say
In accordance with Rabbi Neḥemya, who said indispensable, everyone agrees that is indispensable.

When they disagree, it is in accordance with Rabbi Yehuda, The one who said is not indispensable in accordance with Rabbi Yehuda. And the one who said is indispensable it is different here, because the verse repeated “which came up” “which came up” two times.
raises an objection It is a mitzva to draw and if did not draw the lots is valid.
Granted, according to that version in which you said: In accordance with Rabbi Yehuda everyone, agrees that is not indispensable, whose is this It is Rabbi Yehuda,
